name: Omakase By Aung
tagline: Burmese-Japanese chef, 8 seats, no menu — just trust and exceptional fish.
mustOrder: omakase — there is no menu, the chef decides based on what's fresh
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave; side streets off Winam Ave
talkStory: Chef Aung brings a Burmese-Japanese perspective to edomae-style sushi in an intimate counter setting. About 8–10 seats, direct interaction with the chef throughout the meal. More personal and more 'insider' than Sushi Ginza Onodera (which is a global chain). Reserve ahead — seatings fill up. The experience runs $100–200+ depending on the day's catch. Kapahulu location keeps it off the tourist radar. If you want world-class omakase without Waikiki prices and crowds, this is the move.
